questions.q: In which city of Tamil Nadu does the Brihadeeswarar Temple stand?
questions.explanation: The Brihadeeswarar Temple is located in Thanjavur (Tanjore) in the state of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: To which Hindu deity is the Brihadeeswarar Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva, worshipped here in the form of a colossal lingam.

questions.q: Which Chola emperor built the Brihadeeswarar Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple was commissioned by Raja Raja Chola I, the great Chola emperor, as his royal temple.

questions.q: Which ruling dynasty was responsible for building the Brihadeeswarar Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple was built by the Chola dynasty, which reached its peak power under Raja Raja Chola I.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of the Brihadeeswarar Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is built almost entirely of granite, an enormous undertaking for its time.

questions.q: In which year was the Brihadeeswarar Temple completed?
questions.explanation: The temple was consecrated and completed around 1010 CE during the reign of Raja Raja Chola I.

questions.q: What international designation does the Brihadeeswarar Temple hold?
questions.explanation: It is inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site as part of the 'Great Living Chola Temples'.

questions.q: The towering structure rising over the sanctum of a South Indian temple like Brihadeeswarar is called the:
questions.explanation: The tower over the sanctum (garbhagriha) in Dravidian temples is the vimana; Brihadeeswarar's is among the tallest.

questions.q: The Brihadeeswarar Temple is celebrated as a masterpiece of which architectural style?
questions.explanation: It is a supreme example of Dravidian (South Indian) temple architecture from the Chola period.

questions.q: Within which Indian state is Thanjavur, the home of the Brihadeeswarar Temple?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur, where the temple stands, is a city in the state of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: By what other name is the Brihadeeswarar Temple commonly known?
questions.explanation: In Tamil the temple is known as Peruvudaiyar Kovil (and also as Rajarajeswaram), meaning the temple of the great lord.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the main vimana (tower) of the Brihadeeswarar Temple?
questions.explanation: The vimana rises to about 66 metres (roughly 216 feet), making it one of the tallest temple towers in the world.

questions.q: What name did Raja Raja Chola I originally give to the temple, after himself?
questions.explanation: Raja Raja Chola I named the temple Rajarajeswaram, after his own title, and the deity Rajarajeswaramudaiyar.

questions.q: The huge monolithic Nandi (bull mount of Shiva) facing the sanctum is carved from a single piece of which stone?
questions.explanation: A massive Nandi carved from a single block of granite faces the sanctum, one of the largest such monoliths in India.

questions.q: Roughly how many years ago was the Brihadeeswarar Temple completed, marking a famous millennium milestone celebrated in 2010?
questions.explanation: Completed around 1010 CE, the temple marked its 1,000th anniversary in 2010.

questions.q: The Brihadeeswarar Temple is grouped with two other monuments under which UNESCO World Heritage listing?
questions.explanation: It is part of the 'Great Living Chola Temples', along with the Gangaikonda Cholapuram and Airavatesvara temples.

questions.q: What is the principal object of worship enshrined in the sanctum of the Brihadeeswarar Temple?
questions.explanation: The sanctum houses one of the largest Shiva lingams in India, several metres tall.

questions.q: The river that flows near Thanjavur and whose delta enriched the Chola heartland is the:
questions.explanation: Thanjavur lies in the fertile Kaveri (Cauvery) delta, the agricultural heart of the Chola realm.

questions.q: What did Raja Raja Chola I record in great detail on the temple walls, making it a rich historical source?
questions.explanation: The temple walls carry extensive inscriptions detailing donations, staff, and administration of the temple.

questions.q: During which broad century was the Brihadeeswarar Temple constructed?
questions.explanation: The temple was built in the early 11th century CE, with completion around 1010.

questions.q: The Brihadeeswarar Temple is renowned for early examples of which painted art form on its inner walls?
questions.explanation: The temple preserves rare Chola-period frescoes (murals), some later overlaid by Nayaka-era paintings.

questions.q: Which son of Raja Raja Chola I succeeded him and later built the related temple at Gangaikonda Cholapuram?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I, son of Raja Raja Chola I, succeeded him and built the temple at Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.q: What is the approximate weight of the great capstone (kumbam/shikhara) crowning the vimana?
questions.explanation: The granite capstone atop the vimana weighs roughly 80 tonnes, an extraordinary engineering feat for the 11th century.

questions.q: In which regnal year of Raja Raja Chola I do inscriptions record that the temple was consecrated?
questions.explanation: Inscriptions record that the temple was consecrated on the 275th day of the 25th regnal year of Raja Raja Chola I, around 1010 CE.

questions.q: The temple's main vimana is notably built without which feature seen on many later South Indian temple towers?
options: A towering entrance gopuram taller than the vimana
questions.explanation: Unusually, the central vimana dominates the complex and rises higher than the gateway gopurams, unlike later temples where the gopuram towers over the vimana.

questions.q: Which later rulers of Thanjavur carried out extensive repairs and additions to the temple, recorded in an inscription of 1801?
questions.explanation: The Marathas of Thanjavur preserved the temple and, per an 1801 inscription, repaired several shrines, mandapas and the prakara walls.

questions.q: What architectural innovation lets the towering vimana cast almost no shadow on the ground at certain times, per popular tradition?
questions.explanation: The vimana's precise tapering proportions gave rise to the celebrated tradition that its shadow scarcely falls on the ground at noon.

questions.q: The Chola bronzes and ritual images associated with the temple were chiefly cast using which ancient metalworking technique?
questions.explanation: Chola bronzes were made by the lost-wax (cire perdue) casting method, producing the era's renowned metal images.

questions.q: Carved in the passage above the sanctum, the temple depicts 81 of the 108 karanas (dance poses) linked to which classical Indian dance tradition?
questions.explanation: A frieze above the sanctum shows 81 of the 108 karanas, the dance poses described in Bharata's Natyashastra, the basis of Bharatanatyam.

questions.q: How was the enormous granite for the temple, including the capstone, most plausibly hauled to height, according to traditional accounts?
options: Up a long inclined earthen ramp
questions.explanation: Tradition holds the massive stones, including the capstone, were dragged up a long inclined ramp, a technique consistent with ancient megalithic engineering.
